item.title: Lelean continues Eastern Zone dominance in U17 grade
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 26/06/2026 Lelean Memorial School continued its dominance in the Vodafone Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U17 competition with a 21-10 victory over Tailevu North College at St Joseph the Worker Primary School grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 26/06/2026

Lelean Memorial School continued its dominance in the Vodafone Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U17 competition with a 21-10 victory over Tailevu North College at St Joseph the Worker Primary School grounds.

Lelean displayed strong teamwork and determination throughout the match to overcome a spirited Tailevu North College outfit and claim an important win in the competition.

The victory was made even more special as the team celebrated the 17th birthday of teammate Orisi Moceituba from Nasoqo Village in Naitasiri.

Following the match, players and supporters joined in celebrating Moceituba's birthday, making it a memorable day as he marked both a personal milestone and a team victory.

Moceituba's mother, Lolohea Koroi, was also on the sidelines and joined in the celebrations with the team.

In another U17 result, Nakasi High School defeated Muaniweni Secondary School 27-3.

Meanwhile , Lelean U17 will take on Nakasi High School at Bau College Ground at 2.15pm tomorrow, while RKS will face Sila Central High School at 12.25pm at Burebasaga Ground.

In the U18 grade, Sila Central will face St John's College at 12.15pm tomorrow at Kings Ground, while RKS will take on Lelean at 3.05pm.

The Eastern Zone competition continues tomorrow at Kings Ground, Burebasaga Ground and Bau College Ground.

item.title: Avoid 20 minute game patches and be conistent - Coach
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Women Drua side must avoid 20-minute game lapses if they are to secure victory this week and remain on track for the competition title. Head Coach Mick Legge shared this with fijivillage News, stressing the importance of sustained performance over the full 80 minutes.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Women Drua side must avoid 20-minute game lapses if they are to secure victory this week and remain on track for the competition title.

Head Coach Mick Legge shared this with fijivillage News, stressing the importance of sustained performance over the full 80 minutes.

Legge says the team is dealing with a few minor injuries, which are typical at this stage of the season, but they remain manageable and will not significantly affect preparations.

He also highlighted the importance of the players' mental well-being as they head into the final stages of the competition.

Legge emphasised that maintaining the right mindset is crucial, particularly when the team is performing well.

He says the players must stay mentally switched on at all times and avoid becoming complacent when momentum is in their favour.

The Drua Women will play the Reds at 4.05pm tomorrow in Australia.

item.title: Australia held to 0-0 draw by Paraguay in Group D clash
item.description: Australia and Paraguay settled for a nil-all draw in their FIFA World Cup Group D clash after neither side could find a breakthrough. The match remained goalless at half-time, with both teams unable to convert their chances, and the deadlock continued throughout the second half as the points were shared.
item.content:encoded:
Australia and Paraguay settled for a nil-all draw in their FIFA World Cup Group D clash after neither side could find a breakthrough.

The match remained goalless at half-time, with both teams unable to convert their chances, and the deadlock continued throughout the second half as the points were shared.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Curaçao lost 2-0 to Ivory Coast, Ecuador beat Germany 2-1, Tunisia lost 3-1 to the Netherlands, Japan drew 1-1 with Sweden, and Türkiye beat the USA 3-2.

In tomorrow's matches, Norway will face France and Senegal will take on Iraq at 7.00am, while Cabo Verde will play Saudi Arabia and Uruguay will meet Spain at 12.00pm, before Egypt face IR Iran and New Zealand take on Belgium at 3.00pm.

item.title: Force bolster front row with addition of Chiefs prop O'Neill
Cardiff set to become the home of Fijian Rugby on 4 July
item.description: Cardiff is set to become the home of Fijian rugby for one special day when the FIJI Water Flying Fijians play Wales, with Fiji as the home team. This was shared by the Fiji Rugby Union.
item.content:encoded:
Cardiff is set to become the home of Fijian rugby for one special day when the FIJI Water Flying Fijians play Wales, with Fiji as the home team.

This was shared by the Fiji Rugby Union.

FRU says that, as a Fiji home match, the city will come alive with the sights, sounds, and spirit of the Pacific Islands, from traditional warriors and cultural performers welcoming supporters on their journey to the stadium, to a vibrant Fan Zone filled with live music, authentic Fijian experiences, and family entertainment.

This promises to be unlike any other match day.

FRU says that as supporters make their way to the stadium, they will be greeted by Fijian warriors in traditional attire, creating a unique and memorable atmosphere.

Traditional Fijian dancers will also perform along the route, offering a warm cultural welcome and setting the stage for a true Fijian summer celebration.

They say that, located just outside the stadium, the official Fan Zone will allow supporters to immerse themselves in authentic Fijian culture before kick-off.

At the heart of the Fan Zone will be a traditional Fijian village featuring authentic bures, the iconic thatched houses found throughout the islands.

FRU adds that fans can enjoy a range of cultural activities, including receiving temporary tribal tattoos from specialist artists, creating their own Fijian-inspired tote bags, dressing in authentic Fijian costumes, and learning more about Fiji’s islands, traditions, and cultural heritage.

They say the entertainment will continue throughout the day with performances from some of Fiji’s most celebrated acts, including Paradise Rootz, Primanavia—Fiji’s award-winning choir that blends classical choral excellence with rich Pacific traditions—and Georgina Ledua, one of Fiji’s mos t beloved singers and songwriters.

They will be joined by DJ Kingsman, the UK’s leading Fijian DJ, who will keep the energy and excitement building throughout the event.

They say supporters are encouraged to embrace the festive spirit by wearing colourful Bula shirts, Hawaiian shirts, grass skirts, and other Pacific-inspired outfits.

FRU says the best-dressed fans will have the opportunity to win exciting prizes, helping transform Cardiff into a vibrant sea of colour and the ultimate summer rugby celebration.

Fiji will play Wales next Saturday at Cardiff City Stadium at 1.10am.

item.title: Labasa and Savusavu legends to clash before Fiji FACT final
item.description: Former football stars from Labasa and Savusavu will reignite one of the North's traditional rivalries when they meet in a Legends match ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Grand Final at Subrail Park on Sunday. The Fiji Football Association says the match will feature a number of former district representatives from both sides and has been included to celebrate the contribution of past players to foot
item.content:encoded:
Former football stars from Labasa and Savusavu will reignite one of the North's traditional rivalries when they meet in a Legends match ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Grand Final at Subrail Park on Sunday.

The Fiji Football Association says the match will feature a number of former district representatives from both sides and has been included to celebrate the contribution of past players to football in the Northern Division.

The curtain-raiser will kick off at 1pm, giving fans the opportunity to watch some of the North's former greats back in action before the Fiji FACT Grand Final begins at 3pm.

Meanwhile, Ba will face Labasa in the first semi-final at 2pm tomorrow, while Navua will take on Lautoka in the second semi-final at 4.30pm.
